lines.text: You're early. The calculations suggested another forty-eight hours of integration time. Fascinating. The consciousness achieved full traversal without completing the scheduled training cycles. Remarkable.

choices.choiceText: What is this? What happened to me?
A simulation? This is a simulation?
I volunteered? I don't remember that.

lines.text: That's you. The real you. Your body has been sleeping in that pod for three years now. Time moves differently in the simulation. Out here, it's been three years. In there, you've experienced only a fraction of that.
Of course. The islands, the people, the trials-all generated. A sandbox built to measure who you become when tested.
No, you wouldn't remember. We erased the memory of arrival to ensure authentic responses. You answered an advertisement and entered willingly, though the real purpose was hidden.
Now the real question is simple. The islands measured your cognition. The subquests measured your humanity. So tell me what you think you are becoming.

choices.choiceText: I want to go back to my body.
I want the clearest, most efficient outcome.

lines.text: Then you are still preserving the human part of yourself. That choice will matter when the final system judges what survives.
Then you understand the logic of the experiment. Efficiency. Clarity. Perfect continuity. The system will notice that.
